result.item: AUDIT ITEM 4 — raw-A: checker (evaluator gate) AND entailment (NLI faithfulness) "COMPLETELY off"
result.status: GAP-FOUND — audit claim is HALF WRONG. CHECKER (external evaluator gate): correctly OFF/not-run on raw-A, but this is by design (the evaluator is a separate offline scorer, never part of composition — so "off" is not a defect, it just never was a compose-path gate). ENTAILMENT (NLI faithfulness): the claim is FALSE — entailment/NLI is ON (enforce) on raw-A by default. So the two are NOT both "completely off"; entailment definitely runs.
result.evidence:
CHECKER/EVALUATOR GATE = NOT RUN on raw-A: scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py has 0 imports of external_evaluator (grep count 0); its only post-compose gate is _audit_citations (lines 102-121, 466-472), a pure citation tripwire, not the evaluator. In multi_section_generator.py the ONLY external_evaluator import is at line 8067 (`from ...external_evaluator import _next_real_sentence_end`) — a sentence-boundary helper for the PT11 numeric-cite text transform, NOT evaluate()/score()/verdict. Grep for evaluate(/run_evaluation/.score(/gate_report/EvaluatorResult in the generator returns nothing. Comment at multi_section_generator.py:5297 states outright: "the whole evaluator gate is demoted to advisory in four-role-seam mode".
ENTAILMENT/NLI = ON (enforce) on raw-A: gate flag is PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT with _DEFAULT_MODE = "enforce" at src/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/strict_verify.py:356 (flipped off->enforce per I-bug-095). The compose script NEVER sets it (grep confirms), and no .env/config override exists. verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2561-2622) reads _entailment_mode(); when mode in ("warn","enforce") it calls _get_judge().judge(sentence_clean, combined_span) — a REAL synchronous httpx OpenRouter NLI call (src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py, "synchronous httpx wrapper around an OpenRouter call"). Under enforce (line 2617) a NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED verdict appends "entailment_failed:" (2619-2622) which DROPS the sentence. strict_verify is the production gate for every section via verify_fn=verify_sentence_provenance (multi_section_generator.py:4849) and the evidence-base breadth path calls strict_verify directly (multi_section_generator.py:8218). Entailment judge is the 6th check, runs last after mechanical checks (comment at 2549-2560).
result.codex_verdict: PENDING — Codex (codex-sol) launched via `codex exec ... < /tmp/codex_gates.md` (bg task bdn07hq4y, monitor b467pbsge); had not emitted its GATES-{FULLY-OFF|PARTIAL|ON} token by the time the structured answer was required. Claude's expected Codex verdict on this evidence: GATES-PARTIAL (checker not-run-by-design / entailment ON) — which refutes the "both completely off" claim. Prompt file written and run; token not yet returned.
result.gaps: Audit claim conflates two very different things. (1) The "checker/evaluator gate is off" part is technically true but MISLEADING: the external evaluator was never a composition-time gate — it is an offline report scorer, so its absence on raw-A is expected, not a regression. Calling it "off" implies a disabled safety gate that never existed on this path. (2) The "entailment/NLI is completely off" part is simply WRONG: PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT defaults to "enforce", the compose script does not disable it, and the NLI judge is invoked per kept sentence and drops NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED sentences under enforce. Only remaining unverified nuance: the judge fails OPEN on judge_error (returns ("ENTAILED","judge_error:..."), provenance_generator.py:2591-2596) unless PG_ENTAILMENT_JUDGE_ERROR_ADVISORY is set — so a transport failure can silently pass a sentence, but that is fail-open-on-error, not "off".
result.recommendation: SCOPE ONLY (do not implement): (a) Correct the audit wording — entailment/NLI is NOT off on raw-A; it runs in enforce mode by default. Rephrase item 4 to "checker/external-evaluator gate does not run at compose time (by design — offline scorer); entailment/NLI runs (enforce)." (b) If the intent was to assert the evaluator SHOULD gate composition, that is a design proposal, not a defect finding, and needs separate justification. (c) The one genuine faithfulness-adjacent gap worth a follow-up probe: the entailment judge's fail-open-on-judge_error behavior (PG_ENTAILMENT_JUDGE_ERROR_ADVISORY default off) — quantify how many sentences on a real raw-A run pass via judge_error to confirm it is negligible. Await the Codex GATES-* token (bg task bdn07hq4y) to corroborate before finalizing the audit correction.

result.item: Deliverable Item 5 — single clean reproducible run command: scripts/run_raw_a.sh (tree /home/polaris/wt/phase-file)
result.evidence: Script: /home/polaris/wt/phase-file/scripts/run_raw_a.sh (created; +x). bash -n = SYNTAX_OK; --help = HELP_OK; missing-value guard fires ("option --corpus requires a value"). All 8 required knobs verified against the file: (1) LD_LIBRARY_PATH read via cat from the pinned LDPATH.txt and now exported as EXACTLY the pinned value (line ~93, inherited-append removed) — matches the file's real content; (2) PG_LOOPBACK_MODE=0; (3) PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1, PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK=16, PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True; (4) PG_OUTLINE_MAX_TOKENS=131072, PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS=32768; (5) OPENROUTER_API_KEY + SERPER_API_KEY via python dotenv_values() only, never bash-sourcing .env — verified /workspace/POLARIS/.env line 304 IS `PG_EXA_CATEGORY=research paper` (unquoted space, the documented breakage); (6) unset PYTHONPATH; (7) interpreter /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python; (8) --corpus/--rq-drb-task(=72)/--out-dir flags match driver argparse (compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py lines 172-175) with defaults identical to the proven sibling _run_16way_s3gear329.sh (data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json). Gate did NOT run a paid compose (script exec's driver only on real invocation).
result.gaps: Corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json and DRB query.jsonl (third_party/deep_research_bench/data/prompt_data/query.jsonl) are git-ignored data artifacts not present in a fresh worktree, so the zero-arg default invocation needs the operator to supply a local corpus — this is an environment/data fact (defaults match the proven run verbatim), not a script defect; header now documents it. Script is untracked (git status ??) — expected, since the task scope is CREATE only, not commit.
result.codex_verdict: RUNSCRIPT-REVISE (initial pass on pre-fix script). Codex confirmed knobs 2-7 correct, dotenv used (never sourced), bash -n passes, correct interpreter+flags, no paid run. Its blocking findings: LD_LIBRARY_PATH appended inherited value (real reproducibility bug — FIXED); missing $2 guard (FIXED); knob-8 header lacked per-default rationale (FIXED); corpus/query.jsonl missing + script untracked (environment/scope facts, not changed).
result.recommendation: To reach a re-gate RUNSCRIPT-SOUND, the three script-level fixes are already applied. Remaining to make the default zero-arg run executable end-to-end (out of CREATE scope, do NOT implement here): stage the s3gear-329 corpus + DRB query.jsonl into the worktree (or set --corpus / a data path), and `git add scripts/run_raw_a.sh` if a committed reproducible entrypoint is desired. A quick optional re-run of the codex gate on the revised script would confirm SOUND.
